subject: Project Management
title: Rapid Application Development
description: A fast breakdown of Rapid Application Development for builders.

hook.text: In 1991, James Martin proposed RAD — build fast prototypes instead of writing long specs.

buildup.text: RAD teams built working prototypes in weeks, showed them to users, and iterated quickly.

discovery.text: Users finally saw software before it was finished — and could say 'that's not what I meant.'

twist.text: RAD was dismissed by traditionalists as 'hacking.' The establishment wanted more process, not less.

climax.text: RAD proved that fast feedback beats perfect planning — a seed that would bloom into Agile.

punchline.text: Show it early. Fix it fast. The Agile idea, before Agile.

quiz.question: What was the core idea behind Rapid Application Development?
options: Build fast prototypes and iterate based on user feedback
